# Extração: Video Ad Scripting Process

- **Fonte:** Video Ad Scripting Process.docx
- **Tipo:** DOCX
- **Tamanho:** 294.1 KB

---

Step 1 of 3 - Note Taking

All I want you to do to get started is make some immediate notes below. Brainstorm and remember, there is no such thing as a “wrong” answer or bad idea. We’ll just pick out the winners later.



Aim

What is your viewer looking for? What does he/she want? What’s the initial benefit your offer brings to the viewer? And why is that immediate benefit so important? So they can…. what exactly??



















Difficulty

What is standing in the way of the viewer achieving their aim? This often the reason(s) why they are searching online. Perhaps they can’t find the answer? Maybe they’ve tried things before and it’s not worked? 

Top Tip - You gain “extra points” if you are able to personify this difficulty (e.g. green bacteria monsters/”Mayhem” form the Allstate TV ads) 





















Understand

If your viewer believes you understand exactly how they feel, they will trust you. So ask yourself, ‘what does if feel like when you’re not able to achieve your aim because of the difficulty?’. Articulate this feeling in such a way that makes the viewer think, “yeah, that’s exactly how I feel!”. 



















Credibility

If you’ve nailed the aim, difficulty and understand sections of the script, your viewer will almost be desperate for you to help them. They just need to feel comfortable that you know what you’re doing. So 1 or 2 sentences that showcase you as the expert will be fine. 



















Action Plan

Now your viewer is open to what you have to say, they need to know you have a plan of action. It needs to be seem simple, clear and easy to do. Even if what you do is uber-complex, you need to break it down into steps that anyone can easily understand. A 3-step or 4-step plan is preferred. 

















Teach

Now you want to pull out some incredible value (tips/advice/perspective shifts) from your action plan. Give them your best stuff because want them to think, “Wow, in just 30-45 seconds I have learned so much, what’s next?! How do I find out more?”.





















Exit

What YouTube friendly offer are you promoting? What are the specific steps they need to take to gain access or buy? What does the viewer stand to gain if they take action? What does the viewer stand the lose if they don’t take action?





























Step 2 of 3 - Drafting The Script

Early script writing is fun. I’ve got you started with a template and “power statements” to help build your script out, but feel free to add creative ideas and more. 



Aim

Did You Know... (questions nearly always command attention)

If you’re looking to...



Difficulty

But here’s the problem… or But there’s a problem isn’t there...  

 

Understand

Look… I/we get it. It can feel ___, because ___.

 

Credibility

As a ____, I have helped… or Here at ___, we have helped...

… and we can help you too.



Action Plan

Now, the way this works is actually pretty simple. Ready to see how? Okay, well first… second… and third...



Teach

Here’s the great thing, each step is really easy to understand and implement. In fact, let me walk you through some of it right now... 



Exit

If you’ve found this useful, you’re going to love what I have for you next…

This [offer] has been purposefully designed so you can [insert benefits] 

To gain access right now, here’s what you’re going to do…[add specific call to action].





















Write your draft script here:





Additional Power Statements

Gain instant trust by using this sentence structure: 

[insert relevant and drawback of your offer] BUT [insert strongest feature & benefit of your offer]

E.g. Instead of saying, “The financial plan looks very positive, but, it may take some time to implement” it’s so much better to say, “It may take some time to implement, but, the financial plan looks very positive.” 



Step 3 of 3 - Getting Creative



Now that you’re done with the first draft and adding power statements, it’s time to zero by condensing and deleting parts until it reads well and flows when you read it aloud. Once it’s down to 90 seconds or less, you’ll be left with a power script. Good luck!
